sentence_reviews.text: Diffusion and masked-generative language models iteratively refine corrupted or partially masked token states instead of committing to a strictly left-to-right sequence.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Li et al. 2022 Diffusion-LM applies diffusion to controllable text generation; Sahoo et al. 2024 MDLM describes masked diffusion language models; Ho et al. 2020 provides denoising diffusion background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Iterative denoising/refinement and masked token states are supported across sources; the contrast with left-to-right generation is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: They expose parallel refinement, editing, and controllable generation patterns unavailable to ordinary causal decoding.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Diffusion-LM supports controllable text generation; MDLM supports masked diffusion language modeling. Packet evidence does not fully establish editing or 'unavailable' patterns.
sentence_reviews.notes: Controllable/non-left-to-right generation is supported; editing and 'unavailable' are too strong without more evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: MDLM describes masked diffusion language modeling; Diffusion-LM provides iterative diffusion/refinement for text generation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported by masked diffusion/diffusion language modeling sources.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Diffusion and masked diffusion language models generate via denoising/refinement rather than strict left-to-right autoregression.
sentence_reviews.notes: The non-autoregressive characterization follows from the denoising/masked formulation, but should be cited to explicit text if stricter verification is needed.

sentence_reviews.text: Discrete denoising schedules, likelihoods, variable length, and serving efficiency remain challenging.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Diffusion-LM and MDLM address discrete text diffusion/masked diffusion and language-modeling effectiveness; the exact list of challenges is broader than the packet evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: Known challenges are plausible, but the full limitation sentence needs direct support.

sentence_reviews.text: Diffusion and masked-generative language models extends or specializes the architecture family Diffusion & score-based models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ho et al. 2020 defines denoising diffusion probabilistic models; Diffusion-LM and MDLM adapt diffusion/masked diffusion to language.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported for diffusion language models; masked-generative models are included via MDLM.

sentence_reviews.text: Diffusion and masked-generative language models extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: MDLM language models are typically transformer-based; packet references include Transformer background, but Diffusion-LM evidence is not solely architectural.
sentence_reviews.notes: Common in current implementations, but not inherent to the diffusion-language objective.

sentence_reviews.text: Diffusion and masked-generative language models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Masked prediction objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: MDLM directly uses masked diffusion language modeling; Diffusion-LM supports denoising/refinement but not necessarily masked prediction.
sentence_reviews.notes: Supported for masked diffusion language models; broader diffusion-LM formulations make this common rather than core.

sentence_reviews.text: Diffusion and masked-generative language models consumes, emits, or organizes information using Tokens, vocabularies & symbolic units.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Diffusion-LM and MDLM are language/text generation models operating over token/text states.

edge_reviews.notes: Language diffusion models specialize diffusion/masked diffusion; core strength is supported.
Transformer implementations are common but not inherent to the model family; core strength is overstated by packet evidence.
Masked diffusion supports the optimizes_for relationship; common strength is appropriate.
Text/token state is inherent to language models; common represents_with edge is supported.
